name: Broccoli Vichyssoise
recipeIngredient: 3 quarts Chicken stock or broth (defatted), divided
3 and 1/2 cups Peeled and diced potatoes (1-1/2 pounds)
1 to 2 teaspoons Finely chopped chives for garnish (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Thoroughly wash leek leaves, which tend to collect sand. Trim off and discard root ends and all but about 1 inch of green tops. Peel off and discard 1 to 2 layers of tough outer leaves. Cut leeks crosswise into 1/2 inch pieces.
2. In a Dutch oven or large saucepan, melt margarine over medium heat. Add leeks and 3 tablespoons of stock; cook, stirring frequently, about 10 minutes, until leeks are tender but not browned. If liquid begins to evaporate, lower heat slightly and add more stock.
3. Add remaining chicken stock, potatoes, broccoli, and pepper. Lower heat, cover, and simmer for about 11-14 minutes, until potatoes and broccoli are tender. Remove pot from heat and let cool slightly.
4. In batches, puree the mixture in a blender on low speed for 10 seconds. Then raise speed to high and puree until completely smooth. Return puree to the pot in which it was cooked.
5. Add milk; stir to mix well. Simmer an additional 4-5 minutes. Refrigerate about 5 hours.
6. Before serving, stir in lemon juice and add salt, if desired. Garnish with chopped chives.
